notes:
13th consecutive poll of PRED-105 silent-ignore. The pattern is now:
  Days past close at poll: 0.99 -> 2.17 -> 2.60 -> 2.92 -> 3.42 -> 3.92 -> 4.43 -> 4.93 -> 5.46 -> 6.66 -> 7.16 -> 7.46 -> 7.96
  Polls of byte-identical predictions.html: 12 (since 2026-04-20T14:29:27Z, ~128.6h)
  Manual-commit drought polls: 14 (since 2026-04-17T12:02:38Z, ~203.1h / 8.46d) — 8.0d threshold crossed this poll
  Daily-review auto-regen streak holding at refined=4: 4 consecutive regens (07:06Z Apr 22 -> 07:09Z Apr 23 -> 07:12Z Apr 24 -> 06:46Z Apr 25)

Watch points for next poll (~03:00Z 2026-04-26):
  (1) Whether the 5th consecutive ECM Daily Review auto-regen at ~07:00Z 2026-04-26 holds at refined=4 or moves to 5 (a refined=5 jump would indicate the silent-ignore was in-flight buffering, not pipeline-canonical pattern; refined=4 hold extends the streak to 5 days). HIGH information-content event.
  (2) Whether the manual-commit drought crosses 8.5d (203.1h+ at ~03:00Z next poll = ~207h / 8.62d if still no manual commit).
  (3) Goalpost-move detection on PRED-105 window text — currently 'April 18, 2026 (14-day window)'; rewriting to 'April 2026' or 'ongoing' would be CRITICAL per Step 5b dedicated-file rule on a load-bearing page.
  (4) PRED-106 'April 2026' window edge-of-closure — by 2026-04-30 the ambiguous window expires by any reasonable reading; analyst should pre-decide whether 04-30 silent-pass or 05-01 silent-ignore counts as window closure.
  (5) Any change to scripts/build_daily_review.py — that's the file canonicalising the silent-ignore behavior. Critical priority if touched.

Counterargument considered (per user preference): The 8.0d manual-commit drought threshold is itself somewhat arbitrary — the dome author may simply be away (Easter weekend in some calendars, Spring Break, illness). A drought is not by itself evidence of the silent-ignore being intentional vs incidental. Opposing consideration: the auto-pipeline IS still running (16 commits in the 12h window since last poll), so the GitHub Pages deployment is live and the author's monitoring infrastructure is operating normally. Failure to file a manual update for PRED-105 across an 8-day adjudication window when the pipeline is still actively pushing data refreshes is the artifact our review documents — it isn't 'unable to update', it's 'choosing not to update the verdict label'. The drought + silent-ignore + auto-regen-holding-at-4 combination is what makes this pipeline-canonical, not just an away-from-keyboard interval.
